evals.prompt: pull my last 20 bookmarks and tell me what themes I've been saving
evals.expected_output: Should use `twitter bookmarks -c -n 20` or `--json`, then categorize the results by theme and present a summary

assertions.text: Uses the twitter bookmarks command with -c or --json flag
Analyzes and categorizes the bookmark content after retrieval

evals.prompt: what are people saying about $TSLA options right now on twitter?
evals.expected_output: Should search Twitter for TSLA options discussion using the search command with latest filter and engagement minimums, then summarize sentiment

assertions.text: Uses twitter search command with $TSLA or TSLA options as query
Excludes retweets or filters for quality (--min-likes or --exclude retweets)
Provides sentiment analysis or summary of the results

evals.prompt: save this tweet thread for me: https://x.com/someuser/status/1234567890
evals.expected_output: Should fetch the full thread with replies, then save as a structured markdown file

assertions.text: Uses twitter tweet command with the URL
Includes -n flag to get replies/thread continuation
Saves output to a file (either --json -o or writes a markdown summary)
Does NOT just print the raw CLI output and call it done — reconstructs or summarizes the thread

evals.prompt: I want to see what @chaikitrader has been posting about momentum strategies lately
evals.expected_output: Should search that user's posts for momentum content or pull their recent posts and filter

assertions.text: Uses twitter search with --from flag OR twitter user-posts command
Focuses on momentum-related content in query or analysis

evals.prompt: check twitter for any breaking news about fed rate decision
evals.expected_output: Should do a latest search for fed rate decision content, prioritizing recency and engagement

assertions.text: Uses twitter search with appropriate query terms
Uses -t latest for recency (breaking news = latest)
Summarizes findings with urgency appropriate to breaking news context
